evals.prompt: I need to verify that https://example.com loads correctly. Take a full-page screenshot and confirm the main heading ('Example Domain') is visible on the page. Save the screenshot to the outputs folder.
evals.expected_output: A Playwright script that navigates to https://example.com, waits for the main heading to be visible, takes a full-page screenshot saved to outputs/example_screenshot.png, and prints confirmation that the heading was found.
expectations: The script waits for a specific element to be visible before acting (not networkidle)
A screenshot is saved to an outputs/ directory
The script wraps browser usage in try/finally to ensure proper cleanup
The script confirms or prints whether the heading 'Example Domain' was found
The file outputs/example_screenshot.png exists on disk with a non-zero file size after execution

evals.prompt: Our local Flask app at http://localhost:5000 is already running. I want to capture all browser console errors and warnings while navigating through it. Please write a Playwright script that captures console messages and saves a log to outputs/console_log.txt.
evals.expected_output: A Playwright script that attaches a console event listener, navigates to localhost:5000, waits for the page to load properly, collects console messages (especially errors/warnings), and writes them to outputs/console_log.txt. The script should NOT use with_server.py since the server is already running.
expectations: The script attaches a page.on('console', ...) event listener BEFORE navigating
The script does NOT attempt to manage the server process (server is already running)
Console messages are saved to a file in the outputs/ directory
The script uses wait_for_selector or similar specific wait (not networkidle)
The script handles browser cleanup with try/finally

evals.prompt: I have a static HTML form I want to test: file:///C:/temp/form.html. Discover all the input fields and buttons on this page so I can write automation for it later. List what you find.
evals.expected_output: Following the decision tree for static HTML: reads the file directly to identify selectors, then writes a Playwright script using file:// URL that discovers and lists all input elements and buttons. Does NOT use with_server.py since it's a local file.
expectations: The decision tree for local HTML file (file://) is followed — attempts to read the HTML file directly first
Uses a file:// URL in the Playwright script (not http://)
The script uses page.locator() or similar to discover inputs and buttons
Does NOT use with_server.py (not needed for static HTML)
Output includes a list or print statement identifying the discovered elements
